pkg/framework/entutils/entcursor.purpose: Ent code-generation extension that attaches a `Cursor(ctx, *pagination.Cursor)` method to every Ent query type whose schema has a `created_at` field, enabling stable cursor-based pagination ordered by `(created_at ASC, id ASC)`. The extension is registered in `openmeter/ent/entc.go` and takes effect only after `make generate`.

patterns.name: Ent Extension + embedded template
patterns.description: The extension struct embeds `entc.DefaultExtension`, implements `Templates()` returning a `gen.MustParse`-d template loaded via `//go:embed cursor.tpl`, and exposes `New() *Extension`. This is the only supported way to add generated methods here.

patterns.name: created_at gate in template
patterns.description: The template only emits `Cursor(...)` for nodes that have a field named `created_at`. Schemas without that field get no method generated.

patterns.name: Deterministic ordering: created_at ASC, id ASC
patterns.description: The generated method always appends `ORDER BY created_at ASC, id ASC`; callers must not add conflicting order clauses before calling `.Cursor()`.

patterns.name: CAST(id AS TEXT) for cursor comparison
patterns.description: The cursor WHERE clause casts `id` to TEXT for the string comparison so it works with ULID/UUID string IDs. New schemas using integer IDs will break this assumption.
patterns.example: b.WriteString("CAST("); b.WriteString(s.C("id")); b.WriteString(" AS TEXT) > "); b.Args(cursor.ID)

patterns.name: Non-nil Items on empty result
patterns.description: Generated code initialises items to an empty slice when nil so JSON serialises as `[]` not `null`.

key_file_guides.role: Go template that generates the `Cursor` method on every qualifying Ent query type at `go generate` time.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Template is rendered once per `$.Nodes` entry; changes affect ALL entities with `created_at`. The CAST approach assumes string IDs — numeric ID schemas will silently produce wrong results.

key_file_guides.role: Registers the template as an Ent extension via `entc.Extension`. Must be referenced in `openmeter/ent/entc.go` to take effect.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Extension must be listed in the `entc.Generate` call inside `openmeter/ent/entc.go`; forgetting this means no cursor methods are generated despite the code existing here.

key_file_guides.role: Integration test using `testutils.InitPostgresDB` against a real Postgres instance; validates first-page, next-page, empty-result, and invalid-cursor paths.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Requires `POSTGRES_HOST=127.0.0.1` and `-tags=dynamic`; uses `pkg/framework/entutils/testutils/ent1/db` as the test schema, not the production schema.

anti_patterns: Adding ORDER BY on a query before calling `.Cursor()` — the template appends its own order and the combined ordering will be undefined.
Using this extension on a schema with an integer ID — the CAST-to-TEXT comparison will produce lexicographic ordering, not numeric.
Editing cursor.tpl without running `make generate` — the generated `Cursor` methods in `openmeter/ent/db/` will be stale.
Calling `.Cursor()` with a zero-value `pagination.Cursor{}` — the generated method calls `cursor.Validate()` and returns an error for invalid cursors.

decisions.decision: Composite `(created_at, id)` cursor key instead of offset pagination
decisions.rationale: Offset pagination drifts under concurrent inserts; a `(time, id)` tuple is stable and avoids COUNT(*) on large tables.

decisions.decision: Gate generation on `created_at` field presence rather than an annotation
decisions.rationale: All domain entities in this repo use the TimeMixin that adds `created_at`; the gate is a zero-config convention.

code_examples.scenario: Using the generated Cursor method to paginate an Ent query

pkg/framework/entutils/entdriver.purpose: Thin wrapper that pairs an `entDialectSQL.Driver` with an `entdb.Client` into a single `EntPostgresDriver` struct, providing the canonical way to construct and close Ent + Postgres connections in all binaries and tests. The `Clone()` method allows isolated clients sharing one connection (used in tests).

patterns.name: NewEntPostgresDriver from *sql.DB
patterns.description: Always construct via `NewEntPostgresDriver(db *sql.DB)` — never instantiate `EntPostgresDriver` directly. This ensures the `entDialectSQL.OpenDB(dialect.Postgres, db)` wrapping is applied consistently.

patterns.name: Clone for isolated clients sharing one connection
patterns.description: Use `Clone()` to create a second `EntPostgresDriver` that shares the underlying `*sql.DB` but has its own `entdb.Client`. Used in tests to give each test an isolated client.

patterns.name: Symmetric Close: client first, driver second
patterns.description: `Close()` closes the `entdb.Client` before the `entDialectSQL.Driver`. Reversing this order or skipping client close leaks Ent connection state.

key_file_guides.role: Sole file in this package — defines `EntPostgresDriver` and `NewEntPostgresDriver`. Imported by `app/common/database.go` for wiring and by `openmeter/testutils` for test DB setup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: `Client()` returns the raw `*entdb.Client`; callers should never call `Close()` on it separately after handing it to Wire — `EntPostgresDriver.Close()` owns the lifecycle.

anti_patterns: Constructing `entdb.NewClient` directly in domain or app code instead of going through `EntPostgresDriver` — bypasses the shared lifecycle management.
Calling `driver.Client().Close()` independently — `EntPostgresDriver.Close()` already does this; double-closing causes errors.
Passing `*entdb.Client` across binary boundaries — the client is not serialisable; use Kafka or HTTP for cross-binary communication.

decisions.decision: Bundle driver and client into one struct
decisions.rationale: Ent requires both to be closed in the right order; bundling prevents callers from forgetting one half.

pkg/framework/entutils/entexpose.purpose: Ent code-generation extension that injects `GetConfig()`, `HijackTx()`, and `NewTxClientFromRawConfig()` into each generated `db` package, enabling the shared cross-client transaction mechanism used by `entutils.TransactingRepo`. This is the foundation of the ctx-propagated transaction pattern throughout all domain adapters.

patterns.name: Ent Extension + embedded template
patterns.description: `entexpose.go` implements `entc.Extension` with `Templates()` returning a `gen.MustParse`-d template from `expose.tpl`. Must be registered in `openmeter/ent/entc.go`.

patterns.name: HijackTx for cross-client transaction sharing
patterns.description: `HijackTx(ctx, opts)` starts a SQL transaction on the client's underlying driver and returns `(ctx, *RawEntConfig, *ExposedTxDriver, error)`. The `RawEntConfig` is passed to `NewTxClientFromRawConfig` in a second client to bind it to the same transaction.

patterns.name: ExposedTxDriver implements Transactable with savepoints
patterns.description: `ExposedTxDriver` exposes `Rollback`, `Commit`, `SavePoint`, `RollbackTo`, and `Release` — all SAVEPOINT SQL is issued via `ExecContext` on the `txDriver`. This is the mechanism that lets `entutils.TransactingRepo` nest transactions.

patterns.name: NewTxClientFromRawConfig reconstructs a Tx with empty hooks/inters
patterns.description: Deliberately creates a `Tx` with empty `hooks` and `inters` structs — the TODO comment notes this means on-rollback/on-commit hooks are ignored for shared transactions.
patterns.example: config := config{driver: cfg.Driver, debug: cfg.Debug, log: cfg.Log, hooks: &hooks{}, inters: &inters{}}

key_file_guides.role: Core template — generates `GetConfig`, `HijackTx`, `NewTxClientFromRawConfig`, and `ExposedTxDriver` into the `db` package. Changes here affect ALL generated Ent packages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The TODO comment: `Tx.onRollback` and `Tx.onCommit` hooks are silently dropped when using shared transactions. Do not rely on Ent commit/rollback hooks in shared-transaction flows.
Must stay in sync with `pkg/framework/entutils` types (`RawEntConfig`, `Transactable`) — the template references them by name directly.

anti_patterns: Starting a transaction with `HijackTx` on a client that already has a `txDriver` — the template explicitly returns an error for nested `HijackTx` calls.
Relying on Ent commit/rollback hooks in shared-transaction paths — `NewTxClientFromRawConfig` initialises empty hooks structs, silently dropping them.

decisions.decision: Expose raw `txDriver` internals via code generation rather than a public Ent API
decisions.rationale: Ent does not provide a first-class API for sharing a transaction across multiple `Client` instances; hijacking the internal `txDriver` is the only way to achieve this without forking Ent.

pkg/framework/entutils/entmixinaccessor.purpose: Ent code-generation extension that generates typed `GetXxx()` getter methods for every field (including the ID) that was added to an entity schema via a mixin, enabling interface-based access to mixin fields (namespace, timestamps, ID) without reflection or direct struct field access.

patterns.name: Ent Extension + embedded template
patterns.description: `entmixinaccessor.go` registers `mixinaccessor.tpl` via `entc.DefaultExtension` + `Templates()`. Register in `openmeter/ent/entc.go`.

patterns.name: MixedIn position gate
patterns.description: Getters are only generated for fields where `$f.Position.MixedIn` is true, i.e., fields contributed by a mixin. Fields defined directly on the schema do not get generated getters.

patterns.name: Nillable fields get pointer return type
patterns.description: If a mixin field is `Nillable`, the getter returns `*<Type>` matching the struct field's actual type.
patterns.example: func (e *{{ $n.Name }}) Get{{ $f.StructField }}() {{ if $f.Nillable }}*{{ end }}{{ $f.Type }} { return e.{{ $f.StructField }} }

patterns.name: ID getter is templated separately
patterns.description: The ID field is not in `$n.Fields`; it is accessed via `$n.ID` and gets its own `GetID()` getter only when `$n.ID.Position.MixedIn` is true.
patterns.example: {{- if and $n.ID $n.ID.Position $n.ID.Position.MixedIn }} func (e *{{ $n.Name }}) GetID() {{ $n.ID.Type }} { return e.ID } {{- end }}

key_file_guides.role: Generates typed getters for mixin-injected fields on every entity. Enables domain adapter code to access e.g. `entity.GetNamespace()` via a shared interface without type-asserting.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only mixin fields get getters. If you add a field directly to a schema and need a getter for interface compliance, add it manually or move it to a mixin.

key_file_guides.role: Extension registration. Single responsibility: mount the template into Ent codegen.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must be included in the `entc.Generate` extension list to have any effect.

anti_patterns: Accessing mixin fields like `namespace` via direct struct field access in interface-based adapter code — use the generated `GetNamespace()` getter so the interface stays stable.
Defining a field directly on a schema when it should be a mixin contribution — direct fields do not get getters from this extension.

decisions.decision: Generate getters only for mixin fields, not all fields
decisions.rationale: Mixin fields (namespace, timestamps, ID) are the ones shared across many entities and needed for generic adapter interfaces; generating getters for every field would bloat the generated code.

pkg/framework/entutils/entpaginate.purpose: Ent code-generation extension that attaches a `Paginate(ctx, pagination.Page)` method to every Ent query type, providing offset-based pagination with an automatic COUNT(*) and guaranteed non-nil Items slice. Generated for ALL entity types unconditionally (unlike entcursor which gates on `created_at`).

patterns.name: Ent Extension + embedded template
patterns.description: `paginate.go` registers `paginate.tpl` following the same pattern as all entutils extensions.

patterns.name: Paginate is generated for ALL nodes (no gate)
patterns.description: Unlike entcursor, the paginate template generates `Paginate(...)` for every entity, regardless of fields present.

patterns.name: Clone for COUNT, original for data
patterns.description: The template clones the query for the COUNT(*) call (clearing fields and order) then runs the data query on the original. Callers must not mutate the query after passing it to `Paginate`.

patterns.name: Zero-value Page returns all items
patterns.description: If `page.IsZero()` is true, limit=totalCount and offset=0 — every item is returned with `TotalCount` populated.

patterns.name: Non-nil Items on empty result
patterns.description: Returns `make([]*Node, 0)` when count==0 so JSON serialises as `[]` not `null`.

patterns.name: Static Paginator interface type-check
patterns.description: Each generated `Paginate` method is validated against `pagination.Paginator[*Node]` via a compile-time `var _ =` assertion.

key_file_guides.role: Generates `Paginate(ctx, pagination.Page)` on every Ent query type. The two-query pattern (COUNT + data) means 2 DB round trips per paginated call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Ordering applied before `.Paginate()` is preserved for data query but stripped for COUNT. Callers must apply ordering before calling Paginate, not after.
Must be registered in `openmeter/ent/entc.go` alongside the other entutils extensions.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests covering first page, ordering, filtering, multi-page, empty page, and zero-page (return all) scenarios against real Postgres.

anti_patterns: Applying `.Limit()` or `.Offset()` before `.Paginate()` — the template resets both to 0 on entry, discarding any caller-set values.
Using `Paginate` for large unbounded exports — the zero-page path fetches ALL rows into memory; use cursor pagination via `entcursor` instead.
Relying on COUNT accuracy under high concurrency — the two-query pattern has a TOCTOU gap; TotalCount may differ slightly from Items length.

decisions.decision: Two-query approach (COUNT + data) rather than SQL window function
decisions.rationale: Ent's query builder does not expose a window function API; a clone-and-count is the simplest correct approach with the existing Ent abstraction.
Offset pagination requires no specific schema field (unlike cursor pagination's `created_at` requirement), so there is no meaningful gate.

code_examples.scenario: Offset-paginated list of entities with ordering and filtering
